content.problemStatement:
There are NNN events (numbered from 111 to NNN) and MMM contestants (numbered from 111 to MMM). For each event iii, contestant jjj receives a predetermined number of points Ai,jA_{i, j}Ai,j‚Äč.

A subset of these events will be selected, and the final score of contestant jjj, denoted sjs_jsj‚Äč, will be the sum of their points for each event selected.

Formally, if SSS denotes the set of events chosen, then sj=‚ąĎx‚ąąSAx,j\displaystyle s_j = \sum_{x \in S} A_{x, j}sj‚Äč=x‚ąąS‚ąĎ‚ÄčAx,j‚Äč for each 1‚Č§j‚Č§M1 \leq j \leq M1‚Č§j‚Č§M. If SSS is empty, we define sj=0s_j = 0sj‚Äč=0 for each jjj.

The differentiation of the final scoreboard is equal to

max‚Ā°i=1M(‚ąĎj=1M‚ą£sj‚ąísi‚ą£)\max_{i=1}^M \left(\sum_{j=1}^M |s_j - s_i|\right)i=1maxM‚Äč(j=1‚ąĎM‚Äč‚ą£sj‚Äč‚ąísi‚Äč‚ą£)Find the maximum possible differentiation over all subsets of events.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of several lines.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM ‚ÄĒ the number of events and contestants, respectively.
The next NNN lines describe the events. The iii-th of these NNN lines contains MMM space-separated integers Ai,1,Ai,2,‚Ä¶,Ai,MA_{i, 1}, A_{i, 2}, \ldots, A_{i, M}Ai,1‚Äč,Ai,2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,Ai,M‚Äč ‚ÄĒ the jjj-th of which describes the number of points the jjj-th contestant earns for event iii.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the maximum differentiation over all subsets of events.
constraints: The sum of N‚čÖMN\cdot MN‚čÖM over all test cases won't exceed 10510^5105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: There are two possible subsets of events ‚ÄĒ either take the only event, or don't. In either case, the differentiation will be 000.
Test case 222: Take events 222 and 333 for a final scoreboard of (2,4,2)(2, 4, 2)(2,4,2). The differentiation of this scoreboard is 444, and it can be proven that this is the maximum.
Test case 333: Take events 333 and 555, for a final scoreboard of (7,2)(7, 2)(7,2), which has a differentiation of 555.
